events.title: House of Denmark
events.description: The annual lawn program is held on the first Sunday in June each year. As the 4th of June is “Grundlovsdag” the day the Danish Constitution was signed and transferring the oldest Kingdom in the World from a Monarchy rule of law to a Parlamentary rule of law. events.title: Ted Talks Padres Baseball: An Evening with Ted Leitner
events.description: Spend an unforgettable evening with Ted Leitner—the legendary voice of the San Diego Padres—as he shares stories from a lifetime behind the microphone. Hosted by the San Diego History Center, this lively, free-flowing conversation will feature Ted at his very best: candid, witty, and wonderfully unpredictable. events.title: San Diego’s Underwater Forests with Oriana Poindexter
events.description: Discover the fascinating world of seaweed forests on a visual adventure with artist and marine scientist, Oriana Poindexter. Seaweed forests are some of the most incredible habitats on Earth, and yet these powerful sanctuaries are often overlooked and understudied. That’s where Poindexter comes in. events.title: RITZ – Rendezvous In The Zoo
events.description: Join us for Rendezvous In The Zoo (R·I·T·Z) as we continue to grow our renowned learning programs that inspire explorers to uncover the natural world around them. events.title: Measure for Measure
events.description: Justice and desire collide in Shakespeare’s timeless tale of power and hypocrisy . When Vienna’s Duke suddenly disappears, his morally rigid deputy Angelo takes control—determined to rid the city of vice. events.title: Hello Japan! Language & Culture
events.description: A perfect starting point for new learners who want both language skills and cultural understanding. Students will learn simple grammar, paired with cultural lessons about food, festivals, manners, and modern life in Japan. events.title: House of Philippines Lawn Program
events.description: The yearly lawn program is held on June 12th each year, or the date that is closest to that day. It is a National Holiday — the Independence Day. The Philippine Islands were liberated from Spain in 1898 on that day. The entertainment consists of dancing by a Philippine dance group, singing by choral groups and performances by community celebrities.
events.venue: House of Pacific Relations International Cottages

events.title: Let’s Learn Japanese Letters
events.description: A perfect starting point for new learners who want to learn Japanese letters. Students will learn three types of alphabets used in Japan: Hiragana, Katakana, and Kanji. events.title: Umeboshi Making Class
events.description: Austin Durant from Fermenters Club will teach you how to make Umeboshi from scratch. Umeboshi are traditional Japanese pickled, salt-cured plums. This class will be in two parts. events.title: Brunch at The Nat

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. events.venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)

events.title: Cyrano
events.description: Brilliant poet, dazzling swordsman, and hopeless romantic, Cyrano is as quick with a quip as he is with a blade. But behind his bluster lies shame about his physical appearance. events.title: Pride 5K Run + Walk
events.description: The Pride 5K Run & Walk is always a highlight of Pride Week festivities. As many as 1,700 runners and walkers, from around the world, participate and raise approximately $40,000 for our charity partners. Every year, funds raised at the San Diego Pride 5K generate significant donations for our charity partners – San Diego Pride and The LGBT Center’s Youth Hou

events.title: Fleet Accessibility Mornings
events.description: Early access to galleries at 9 a.m. On the third Saturday of every month, we open our doors one hour before the museum opens to the public and screen a documentary with house lights on and the soundtrack volume low. events.title: Cars and Coffee
